# Package P0009 Implementation Design

## Package Interpretation

P0009 changes the Shelly build tool so a built script is no longer plain concatenated source. The tool must generate a deterministic complete script containing a generated-file header, role/manifest/source/tool metadata, an IIFE wrapper, and `"use strict";` inside the wrapper.

Generated deploy chunks and recipes must be rebuilt from the new complete built script and must still validate by exact reconstruction.

## Metadata Format

The generated header will use JavaScript line comments:

```js
// Generated by G2 Shelly build tool. Do not edit manually.
// g2_role=<role>
// g2_manifest=<manifest path as supplied to the build>
// g2_sources=<comma-separated source paths from manifest>
// g2_tool=src.mac.tools.shelly_build
// g2_package=P0009
```

The manifest path will be normalized with POSIX separators for deterministic output across path object/string inputs.

## Wrapper Format

The assembled source will be wrapped as:

```js
(function () {
  "use strict";

<source content>
}());
```

Source content is indented by two spaces inside the wrapper. Blank lines remain blank. The wrapper is deterministic and keeps original source text order from the manifest.

## Intended Changes

- Change `src/mac/tools/shelly_build/core.py`:
  - add helper functions for deterministic metadata, source assembly and wrapper formatting
  - change `build_script()` to return wrapped generated output
  - preserve existing validation, chunking, recipe and CLI behavior
- Change `tests/mac/tools/shelly_build/test_core.py`:
  - verify generated header, role metadata, manifest metadata, source metadata, IIFE wrapper and strict mode
  - keep existing P0008 tests passing
- Rebuild generated artifacts:
  - `build/shelly/fixture/hello.js`
  - `dep/s/ch/hello/01.js`
  - `dep/s/rec/hello.json`
- Update durable documentation:
  - `memory/device-management/source-build-deploy-layers.md`
  - `docs/functions/mac/shelly-build-tool.md`
- Fill P0009 package completion notes after verification.

## Files Intentionally Not Changed

- No G1 repository files.
- No live device state.
- No installer implementation.
- No real FTX runtime source.
- No external dependencies.
- No package template/process changes.

## Refactoring Decisions

Small private helpers are added in `core.py` because wrapper/header assembly needs clear deterministic units and focused tests. No broad module split or CLI redesign is planned.

## Test Strategy

Run:

```bash
python3 -m unittest discover tests/mac/tools/shelly_build
python3 -m src.mac.tools.shelly_build build --manifest src/shelly/fixture/manifest.json --build-root build/shelly/fixture --dep-root dep/s
python3 -m src.mac.tools.shelly_build validate --build-root build/shelly/fixture --dep-root dep/s --role hello
git diff --check
```

Also inspect `git status` and confirm the diff is inside P0009 allowed files before commit/push.

## Risks And Uncertainties

- IIFE wrapping changes top-level source scope. That is intentional for future Shelly scripts, but P0009 only verifies the fixture and does not run on live Shelly.
- The metadata format is newly defined here. Future packages may refine it if installer/version semantics require more fields.
